title: eriogonum grande var. timorum
intro: Eriogonum grande var. timorum, also known as the desert trumpet, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the buckwheat family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum grande var. timorum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tea or tincture, can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
